channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Elon Musk entertains idea of buying MSNBC
item.description: Elon Musk is flirting with the idea of buying MSNBC amid speculation the left-leaning cable news network could be put up for sale.
item.dc:creator: Brian Niemietz, The Mercury News

item.title: Trace3 names Jason Peoples as Outlier Award winner for 2024
item.description: Innovators have the unique ability to see what’s possible, bringing together in new ways, acclimating to change and thriving within it, and creating true transformation. Few people are true innovators, but it’s those characteristics that make an innovator worthy of the title “Outlier.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Viajes aéreos 100% digitales, seguros y personalizados gracias a la biometría
item.description: Imagínese una experiencia paperless en los aeropuertos, donde la fricción se reduzca considerablemente y la experiencia del viajero relegue al pasado el estrés y la tensión.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Judge hears closing arguments on whether Google's advertising tech constitutes a monopoly
item.description: The Justice Department and Google have made their closing arguments in a trial alleging Google’s online advertising technology constitutes an illegal monopoly.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Barakat, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: El despliegue de ChatGPT Enterprise en BBVA desata una gran ola de innovación
item.description: Hace ya unos meses que BBVA, la entidad financiera española con más de 167 años de historia a sus espaldas, está sentando las bases para afrontar el gran reto que supone la aplicación de la inteligencia artificial (IA) generativa al sector bancario.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: JDK 24: The new features in Java 24
item.description: In the wake of Java Development Kit (JDK) 23, which arrived September 17, work now focuses on the planned successor release, JDK 24, which has ballooned to 24 proposed features. The four most recent additions include warnings upon the use of memory-access methods in sun.misc.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Hyundai, Kia recall over 208,000 electric vehicles to fix problem that can cause loss of power
item.description: The affiliated Korean automakers say in government documents that a transistor in a charging control unit can be damaged and stop charging the 12-volt battery.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Thai court dismisses activist's suit against Israeli spyware producer over lack of evidence
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — A Thai court on Thursday threw out a lawsuit brought by a pro-democracy activist which alleged spyware produced by an Israeli tech firm had been used to hack his phone.
item.dc:creator: JERRY HARMER and DAVID RISING, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: AI-powered dog robot sniffs out invasive fire ants
item.description: Fueled by artificial intelligence, CyberDog the robotic dog is designed specifically locate fire ant nests, which are endangering ecosystems.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Azure Container Apps launches Python, JavaScript interpreters
item.description: Python code interpreter sessions are generally available for Microsoft’s Azure Container Apps serverless platform for running applications and microservices. The service is also offering custom dynamic sessions, with JavaScript code interpreter capabilities in public preview.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: House lawmaker wants to do away with Pentagon’s CMMC program
item.description: Alabama Republican Gary Palmer has submitted a joint resolution of disapproval to overturn the CMMC rule. The post House lawmaker wants to do away with Pentagon’s CMMC program first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Michele Sandiford, Federal News Network

item.title: Top 5 fastest-growing cities for data centers
item.description: The five fastest-growing hubs for data center expansion include an interesting mix of urban areas that have one thing in common: lots of available power. Based on projected data-center capacity growth, Las Vegas/Reno is the No.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Apple admins: Update your hardware now
item.description: Among the first things Apple IT admins woke up to this morning was news of a pair of actively exploited zero-day attacks in the wild targeting Intel Macs, iPhones, iPads, and even Vision Pro users.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Australia won't force social media users to share their personal details when child ban takes effect
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — Australia's communications minister said Wednesday the government won't force social media users to hand over their personal information to tech companies, as children younger than 16 are set to be banned from the platforms.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Microsoft extends Entra ID to WSL, WinGet
item.description: Microsoft has added new security features to Windows Subsystem for Linux (WSL) and the Windows Package Manager (WinGet), including integration with Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Active Directory) for identity-based access control.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Microsoft pitches AI 'agents' that can perform tasks on their own at Ignite 2024
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella told customers at a conference in Chicago on Tuesday that the company is teaching a new set of artificial intelligence tools how to "act on our behalf across our work and life.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Cato Networks adds Safe TLS inspection capabilities to SASE platform
item.description: Secure access service edge provider Cato Networks today announced it added safe transport layer security inspection capabilities to its SASE platform to enable customers to inspect TLS traffic more easily and quickly.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Linux command line for beginners: 12 essential commands
item.description: Getting started on the Linux command line might seem overwhelming at first, but the many commands you need to use will fall into place more quickly than you might imagine. Let’s start with some introductory but very important commands.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Forget driverless cars. One company wants autonomous helicopters to spray crops and fight fires
item.description: HENNIKER, N.H. (AP) — When Hector Xu was learning to fly a helicopter in college, he recalled having a few “nasty experiences” while trying to navigate at night.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: This tool is made for the laziest parent ever
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson discusses how an innovative Easy-Way kit turns standard strollers electric, simplifying navigation for parents.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: German police break up an environmental activists' camp near Tesla plant outside Berlin
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — German police broke up a protest camp housing environmental activists in a forest near the Tesla electric car factory in Grünheide outside Berlin on Tuesday, citing violations of public safety and order.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: How to transform your architecture review board
item.description: I recall my angst about my first visit to an architecture review board (ARB), where a team of enterprise architects reviewed plans for new systems and application architectures.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: A GRC framework for securing generative AI
item.description: From automating workflows to unlocking new insights, generative AI models like OpenAI’s GPT-4 are already delivering value in enterprises across every industry.
item.dc:creator: Info World
